OPPOSITION TO MTN TO DISMISS OR STAY FILED BY DEFENDANT EXCALIBUR REINSURANCE CORPORATION FKA PMA CAPITAL INSURANCE COMPANY, AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO CALIBER ONE INDEMNITY COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CROSS DEFENDANT ACCEPTANCE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. AIC HAS BEEN IN REHABILITATION IN NEBRASKA FOR FIVE YEARS. EVEN IF NEBRASKA IS A RECIPROCAL STATE UNDER THE UILA FOR PURPOSES OF CALIFORNIA LAW, DISMISSAL OR STAY OF THIS PARTICULAR ACTION IS NOT WARRANTED. THE UILA DOES NOT PROHIBIT A PARTY FROM MAINTAINING A CALIFORNIA ACTION TO OBTAIN A JUDGMENT AGAINST AN INSURER THAT IS IN INSOLVENCY OR REHABILITATION PROCEEDINGS ELSEWHERE. (SEE HAWTHORNE SAV. F.S.B. V. RELIANCE INS. CO. OF ILL. 421 F. 3D 835, 855-56 (9TH CIR. 2005.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Mtn To Dismiss
Off Calendar Apr-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JENNIFER KALVESTRAN, ESQ., 213.629.7849, FOR PLAINTIFF ARROWWOOD SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY.
PETER DYE, ESQ., 310.445.0800, FOR DEFENDANT EXCALIBUR REINSURANCE CORPORATION.
MICHAEL OLSON, ESQ., 949.442.8940, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT ACCEPTANCE INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT ACCEPTANCE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION To Dismiss is denied. AIC has been in rehabilitation in Nebraska for five years. Even if Nebraska is a reciprocal state under the UILA for purposes of California law, dismissal or stay of this particular action is not warranted. The UILA does not prohibit a party from maintaining a California action to obtain a judgment against an insurer that is in insolvency or rehabilitation proceedings elsewhere. (See Hawthorne Sav. F.S.B. v. Reliance Ins. Co. of Ill. 421 F. 3d 835, 855-56 (9th Cir. 2005.) Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Mtn To Dismiss
Off Calendar Apr-03-2013 Continued To Apr-17-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JENNIFER C. KALVESTRAN, ESQ., 213.629.7849, FOR PLAINTIFF ARROWOOD SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY.
PETER W. DYE, ESQ., 310.445.0800, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT EXCALIBUR REINSURANCE CORPORATION.
MICHAEL C. OLSON, ESQ., 949.442.8940, FOR CROSS-DEFENDANTS ACCEPTANCE INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING: MATTER ARGUED. AT CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT CONTINUES HEARING ON CROSS DEFENDANT ACCEPTANCE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Dismiss TO APRIL 17, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. THE COURT ORDERS MOVING PARTY TO FILE/SERVE BY EMAIL 5 PAGE BRIEF NO LATER THAN APRIL 5, 2013 AND OPPOSING PARTY TO FILE/SERVE BY EMAIL 5 PAGE BRIEF NO LATER THAN APRIL 11, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Mtn To Dismiss
